profile.name: Tobiloba Awolaju
profile.title: Web3 Systems Engineer | Blockchain Infrastructure
profile.bio:
I design and build distributed Web3 systems including multi-node blockchain indexers, reorg-aware ingestion pipelines, and hybrid on-chain/off-chain architectures.

I am a final-year Electrical & Electronic Engineering student at Adeleke University (Expected 2026), with academic focus in Digital Communications, Signals & Systems, Control Systems, and Embedded Systems. My engineering foundation informs my approach to reliability, concurrency control, and scalable backend architecture for crypto-native teams.

sectionLabels.projectsDescription: Production-style blockchain infrastructure, distributed ingestion pipelines, and hybrid Web3 systems.

projects.title: MonadTraceEngine ↗
projects.description: 3-node EVM RPC ingestion engine with consensus comparison, reorg detection, token-bucket rate limiting (40 req/sec/node), rollback handling, and real-time execution trace streaming via WebSockets.
projects.tech: Node.js, WebSockets, EVM RPC, Firebase RTDB

projects.title: WorldOfNads ↗
projects.description: Hybrid Web2/Web3 multiplayer arena game with 20-player real-time synchronization (20Hz server tick) and trustless on-chain match settlement.
projects.tech: TypeScript, Node.js, WebSockets, Godot (WASM), EVM Integration

projects.title: DominusQuant ↗
projects.description: AI-assisted crypto trading infrastructure with modular signal aggregation, behavioral analytics, and optional automated execution architecture with risk guardrails.

docs.title: Optimizing Gas Costs in Solidity ↗
docs.description: Deep dive into EVM storage layout, SLOAD/SSTORE cost mechanics, and gas optimization strategies.

docs.title: ZK-Rollups vs Optimistic Rollups ↗
docs.description: Comparative analysis of Ethereum scaling models, trade-offs, and security assumptions.
